name: Stewed Mushrooms & Queens
recipeIngredient: 1/4 pint Single Cream or Jersey Milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice mushrooms thickly.
2. Place mushrooms in a pan with cold milk (and cream if using). Cover three-quarters of the pan and bring to a fast simmer.
3. Add bay leaf and a pinch of salt, then reduce heat to low. Cover three-quarters again and let stew gently for 5-6 minutes.
4. Strain the liquid from mushrooms, discard the bay leaf, and keep mushrooms warm in a very low oven.
5. Prepare sauce: In a separate pan, melt butter and flour; stir in at least half a teaspoon each of tarragon vinegar and anchovy essence. Simmer for 2-3 minutes before adding salt and pepper to taste.
6. Stir bay scallops into the sauce over low heat, covering halfway and cooking for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally until only occasional bubbles remain.
7. Remove pan from heat, stir in mushrooms, check seasoning, then serve in a warmed gratin dish or divided among scallop shells. Garnish with parsley and fried breadcrumbs. Serve alone as an appetizer, three people for a main course, or six for shared appetizers.
